new_classes.label_en: Still Fermented Beverage from Concentrated Grape Must in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2206 00 51)
new_classes.label_de: Stilles Gärgetränk aus konzentriertem Traubenmost in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (KN 2206 00 51)
new_classes.definition_en: A still (non-sparkling) fermented beverage produced not from the natural fermentation of fresh grape must but from concentrated grape must, presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres. The fermentation process is typically initiated with yeast; sugar may also be added as a fermentation substrate.

new_classes.label_en: Fermentation Process from Concentrated Grape Must
new_classes.definition_en: A fermentation process in which the substrate is concentrated grape must (rather than fresh grape must), typically initiated with yeast and optionally with added sugar, yielding a fermented beverage classified under CN heading 2206.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the three main distinguishing criteria for CN 2206 00 51: (1) the beverage is produced from concentrated grape must (not natural fermentation of fresh grape must) — captured via the ConcentratedGrapeMustFermentationProcess linked to ConcentratedGrapeMust; (2) the beverage is still (non-sparkling) — captured via isCarbonated=false; (3) it is presented in containers ≤ 2 litres — captured via haspart SmallContainer. The legal text also mentions that (4) fermentation is typically started with yeast and (5) sugar may be added as a substrate. These production detail nuances (yeast addition, sugar as fermentable) could not be fully captured without introducing new classes for yeast input and sugar substrate that are not currently in the TBox, so these two sub-criteria reduce the coverage score slightly. The German note references HS Explanatory Note §2206 items 1–10 which is a cross-reference only and not directly axiomatisable.
